title: Observe with Dynatrace - Knowledge Check
description: Confirm you understand how Dynatrace instrumented the app.

questions.title: How does the Todo app get instrumented?
questions.content: summonDynatrace deploys the Operator and a DynaKube, then restarts the Todo app. Why the restart?

options.text: So OneAgent can inject into the new pods automatically (no code changes)
options.explanation: Injection happens when pods are (re)created after the DynaKube is applied.
The restart does not touch app data.

options.text: Because the app crashes without Dynatrace
options.explanation: The app runs fine uninstrumented; the restart is only for injection.

questions.explanation: OneAgent injects into pods created after the DynaKube is applied, so the app is restarted to pick up injection.

questions.title: Which DQL time bound did the log check use?
questions.content: The log verification query filtered timestamps how?

options.explanation: Always time-bound DQL; the check used the last 10 minutes.
Unbounded DQL is discouraged and scans too much.
Far too wide for a freshness check.

questions.explanation: The query bounded to the last 10 minutes to check that logs are flowing now.
hints: Look back at the fetch logs query.
